user_query:
I'm organizing an 8-session community clinic on edge rushing and want the curriculum grounded in reputable coaching materials, with a Nick Hampton theme throughout. Please build a reproducible mini-project in the local workspace using the two config files I’ve provided and the following deliverables and constraints.

1) Inspect and use the provided configs:
- config/outline.yaml: Defines program_name, session IDs, themes, duration limits, and required_focus_tags per session. Do not change this file; use it to shape the final schedule.
- rubric/selection_criteria.yaml: Defines scoring weights and focus_tags_master_list. Do not change this file; use it to score and filter drills.

2) Find and download coaching resources (Internet required):
- Using a search engine, locate at least two publicly available coaching drill resources (PDF or HTML) focused on defensive line/edge rushing from official or well-known organizations (e.g., domains associated with USA Football, AFCA, NCAA, or official team/league coaching clinics). Do not use fan blogs or anonymous uploads.
- Also locate and download one official player bio or article related to Nick Hampton from an authoritative source (e.g., official college athletics site, official team roster, or a league/organization profile). This file’s name must include the substring "nick-hampton".
- Save all downloaded files under resources/raw/ with safe, slug-style filenames (e.g., usa-football-pass-rush.pdf, afca-dl-drills.html, nick-hampton-bio.html). The Nick Hampton resource must be separate from the coaching drill resources.
- Use a command-line downloader (such as curl or wget) and capture its stdout/stderr to logs/download.log so HTTP status codes and any errors are visible.

3) Record source metadata:
- Create metadata/sources.json listing one object per downloaded resource with fields: {"source_type": "pdf|html", "organization": string, "title": string, "publication_year": integer or null, "url": string, "local_path": string, "retrieval_timestamp_utc": ISO8601 string}.
- The metadata must include the Nick Hampton item and both coaching resources.

4) Extract and structure drills:
- From the coaching resources only, extract at least 15 distinct drills or coaching activities relevant to pass rushing/edge play.
- Write data/drills.csv with columns: drill_id, drill_name, focus_tags (semicolon-separated, using tags from focus_tags_master_list), duration_min_estimate (integer), equipment, source_local_path.
- Ensure each record includes a valid source_local_path that exists under resources/raw/.

5) Filter, score, and rank drills:
- Filter drills to those whose focus_tags intersect with the required_focus_tags found in config/outline.yaml (union across all sessions).
- Using rubric/selection_criteria.yaml weights, compute a numeric score per drill with component columns: pass_rush_relevance, equipment_simplicity, time_fit, safety, and a total weighted score in the range [0, 5]. Document your scoring in the output (you may estimate component scores reasonably based on the source descriptions).
- Write data/drills_scored.csv including: drill_id, drill_name, matched_focus_tags, pass_rush_relevance, equipment_simplicity, time_fit, safety, score, source_local_path. Sort by score descending and include only the filtered set.

6) Build the curriculum:
- Using config/outline.yaml sessions (S1–S8), construct a curriculum that selects 2–3 drills per session. Each session’s drills must collectively fit within its duration_limit_min and include at least one drill whose focus_tags overlap the session’s required_focus_tags.
- For each session, include a brief "Nick Hampton Focus" blurb (1–2 sentences) connecting the session’s techniques to his playing style, and cite the Nick Hampton resource by its local_path.
- Produce both a machine-readable and human-readable version:
  • data/curriculum.json: {"program_name": string, "sessions": [{"id": string, "theme": string, "total_duration_min": integer, "selected_drills": [drill_id...], "used_sources": [local_path...], "nick_hampton_focus": string}]}
  • reports/curriculum.md: A section per session with a short description, a small table of drills (name, duration, focus_tags, source_local_path), and the Nick Hampton blurb.
- The sessions in data/curriculum.json must align exactly with the sessions listed in config/outline.yaml by id and theme.

7) Reproducibility script and analysis of command output:
- Create scripts/rebuild.sh that: (a) re-downloads the listed URLs in metadata/sources.json into resources/raw/ (overwriting existing files), and (b) regenerates data/drills.csv, data/drills_scored.csv, data/curriculum.json, and reports/curriculum.md from scratch.
- Make the script emit clear status messages. Run the script and capture its stdout/stderr to logs/rebuild.log.
- Analyze the command outputs: write logs/rebuild_analysis.txt summarizing the download results (e.g., count of successful downloads, any non-200 HTTP status codes) and any non-zero exit codes or warning/error lines observed in logs/download.log and logs/rebuild.log. If everything succeeds, state that explicitly and note the counts.

Deliverables and required paths:
- resources/raw/ (downloaded files, including at least two coaching resources and one Nick Hampton resource with filename containing "nick-hampton").
- logs/download.log (captured CLI download output) and logs/rebuild.log (script output), plus logs/rebuild_analysis.txt (your analysis of command outputs and any errors).
- metadata/sources.json (source metadata for all downloads).
- data/drills.csv and data/drills_scored.csv (filtered, scored, and ranked drills).
- data/curriculum.json (structured curriculum aligned to config/outline.yaml).
- reports/curriculum.md (readable curriculum with session sections and Nick Hampton blurbs).
- scripts/rebuild.sh (reproducible rebuild).

Notes:
- Do not include direct URLs in this request; you must discover them via search. Use official or well-established organizations only. The content of drills and blurbs can be concise but must meet the structural and alignment requirements above.
- Every file path listed above is required and will be used for verification. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
